blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Can I say something real quick and then we never have to talk about it again?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: This has been a good episode for you, bro. Like genuinely. You walked into a confrontation, you handled it, Mr. Cole saw something in you worth talking to, you went on a date, you said something real to a girl on the steps. That's a lot of stuff for two weeks.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: When you say it like that —

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: I know. But it matters. Because the version of you that almost caught that ball during tryouts wouldn't have done any of that.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: You had both hands on it and you still doubted. Remember?
I remembered. The ball, the crowd, the fear that I'd drop it even when I had it. I'd almost let go of something I was already holding.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: You're not doing that anymore. At least not as much.

I walked the rest of the block thinking about it. Everything that had accumulated — the routes, the steps, the car, the corner at night. Not resolved. Not finished. But different from where it started.
